complaint_description: Tablet has unusual smell on opening bottle. Patient reported strong chemical odor not present on previous refills.
root_cause: Packaging seal failure — moisture ingress leading to degradation of active ingredient
capa_summary: Seal integrity test added to QC release protocol. Desiccant quantity increased. Supplier audit scheduled.

complaint_description: Tablet fragmentation observed. Patient reported tablets breaking apart during removal from blister pack.
root_cause: Compression force out of specification during tablet manufacturing — tablets brittle due to low hardness
capa_summary: Compression force limits tightened. In-process hardness testing frequency increased from hourly to every 30 minutes.

complaint_description: Patient developed fever and chills within 30 minutes of IV administration. Suspected particulate contamination.
root_cause: Glass delamination from vial inner surface. Visible glass particles found in returned samples.
capa_summary: Full batch recall initiated. Vial supplier qualification suspended. Alternative borosilicate vial supplier qualified. 100% visual inspection added.

complaint_description: Wrong dosage strength printed on carton label. Carton states 40mg but bottles contain 20mg tablets.
root_cause: Label mix-up at packaging line during changeover from 40mg to 20mg production run. Line clearance procedure not followed.
capa_summary: Line clearance SOP revised with mandatory second-person verification. Electronic label verification system installed.

complaint_description: Discoloration observed — tablets appear yellowish instead of white. Multiple patients reported across same batch.
root_cause: Excipient degradation due to temperature excursion during warehouse storage. Cold chain deviation not logged.
capa_summary: Temperature monitoring upgraded to continuous IoT sensors with automated alerts. Storage area HVAC serviced. Stability protocol updated.

complaint_description: Capsule shell cracking reported by pharmacy. Approximately 15% of capsules in multiple bottles showing cracks.
root_cause: Low humidity in production area causing capsule shells to become brittle during filling operation.
capa_summary: Relative humidity specification in filling suite tightened. Real-time humidity monitoring added to batch record. Capsule supplier notified.

complaint_description: Tablet coating peeling off observed in multiple consumer complaints from same pharmacy chain.
root_cause: Coating solution viscosity out of specification. Incorrect solvent ratio used during preparation.
capa_summary: Coating solution preparation checklist added with mandatory QC sign-off before use. Viscosity in-process test added.

complaint_description: Capsule content clumping reported. Powder inside not flowing freely — patient could not empty capsule.
root_cause: Moisture absorption during granulation step exceeded specification. Batch granulated on day with unusually high ambient humidity.
capa_summary: Dehumidification in granulation suite upgraded. Water activity testing added to granule intermediate release specification.

complaint_description: French language text missing from multilingual label — only English and German present. EU labeling requirement not met.
root_cause: Artwork version control failure. Outdated label template used that predated the French language addition requirement.
capa_summary: Label artwork management system replaced with validated electronic system. All artwork changes require QA electronic approval. Retrospective label audit completed.

complaint_description: Potency out of specification — assay result 88% vs specification of 95-105%. Reported by wholesaler QC lab.
root_cause: Active ingredient degradation due to extended hold time of bulk tablets before packaging. Hold time limit exceeded by 11 days.
capa_summary: Maximum bulk hold time added to batch manufacturing record with mandatory expiry alert. Retrospective stability data reviewed and filed.

complaint_description: Foreign particle (black speck) found inside sealed bottle. Consumer submitted product for analysis.
root_cause: Metal particle from worn compression punch tip. Punch set inspection interval was insufficient.
capa_summary: Punch inspection frequency doubled. Metal detector sensitivity increased. 100% visual inspection of suspect batches. Punch set replaced.

complaint_description: Tablets sticking together inside bottle. Multiple tablets fused at contact points.
root_cause: Tablet coating incomplete — subtherapeutic coating thickness allowed moisture transfer between tablet surfaces during storage.
capa_summary: Coating weight gain specification tightened. Weight gain measured at start, middle and end of coating run. Stability samples added.

complaint_description: Expiry date missing from outer carton. Printed correctly on inner bottle but not on carton.
root_cause: Carton printing head malfunction during production run. Sensor that should detect missing print did not alarm.
capa_summary: Carton print inspection sensor replaced. 100% carton inspection added using camera system. Batch quarantined and relabeled.

complaint_description: Tablet weight variation out of specification found by hospital pharmacy during routine incoming QC check.
root_cause: Hopper feed rate inconsistency during tablet compression. Detected during third-party incoming QC — was not caught at manufacturer.
capa_summary: In-process weight check frequency increased. Automated weight check system qualified and installed on compression line.

complaint_description: Visible white particles observed in insulin pen cartridge. Patient noticed before administration and did not inject.
root_cause: Protein aggregation due to temperature excursion during last-mile delivery. Cold chain break identified in shipping data.
capa_summary: Temperature indicator added to all insulin shipments. Cold chain SOP updated with mandatory temperature check at receipt. Carrier quality agreement revised.

complaint_description: Visible black particulate matter found in heparin injection vials by hospital pharmacy during incoming inspection. Three vials from same batch affected. Product not administered.
root_cause: Rubber stopper fragmentation during vial crimping — excessive crimping force caused stopper particles to shed into solution.
capa_summary: Crimping force specification tightened and validated. 100% automated visual inspection added to release testing. Rubber stopper supplier qualification audit completed. Affected batch recalled.
